PROPOSAL, do not merge: add test discovery
pr_body:
_This PR is for discussion.  If we agree that it's a good direction, I'll put more work into it._

Python unittest has a nice feature of test discovery.  This PR lets me run tests from the project root with `python -m unittest discover`.  This is nice for a few reasons:

- I _believe_ it's a standard for python projects
- good onboarding experience for new developers - tests are up and running with `git clone ...; pip install -r requirements; python -m unittest discover`

A couple of requirements for this to work:

- test directories need `__init__.py`
- test files must be named `test_*.py`

With the changes in this PR (two separate commits), running `python -m unittest discover` from the project root gave me the following:

```
(venv) ~/Documents/Projects/napalm$ python -m unittest discover
EE.E..........E..

ERROR: setUpClass (test.unit.test_ios_driver.TestConfigIOSDriver) ... [snip] ...
NetMikoTimeoutException: Connection to device timed-out: cisco_ios 127.0.0.1:12204
ERROR: test_bgp_neighbors_detail (test.unit.test_ios_driver.TestGetterIOSDriver) ... [snip] ...
NotImplementedError
ERROR: test_get_bgp_config (test.unit.test_ios_driver.TestGetterIOSDriver) ... [snip] ...
NotImplementedError
ERROR: test_get_route_to (test.unit.test_ios_driver.TestGetterIOSDriver) ... [snip] ...
NotImplementedError
```

I'm not concerned about the `NotImplementedError` at this point, though it would be something I'd want to address with a full implementation of this idea, after discussion.

I'd want to do the following for this PR to be considered complete:

- change all of the test files to `test_*.py`
- fix/deal with all of the NotImplementedErrors.  They could either be fixed or swallowed, or the tests could somehow handle them to say that there are things pending implementation.  I don't like hiding errors like that, it's good to keep them public ... but that information could be output to a report and actually committed into the main repo as part of the product roadmap.
- somehow handle the "Connection to device timed-out" - presumably these tests are actually trying to connect to a real thing.  If we're mocking the underlying classes, maybe the tests can be categorized somehow.  When I changed this test to mocking like the other tests, I got the error "No such file or directory: 'ios/initial.conf'".  Not a big deal, just fyi.
- add notes to the README or to a developer README explaining how to run the tests
- maybe add a Makefile to the project root to handle running the tests and dealing with the NotImplementedExceptions report.  It could, for example, run the tests with `--buffer` and then process the completed report.  It could also specify command-line args that would let us toggle between mock devices during tests vs real devices.

I hope to get a chance to discuss this on Slack (I'm on networktocode as jzohrab), and if we agree that this is a good direction I'd be happy to work on getting this fleshed out for all of the classes in `test/unit` and getting the tests to run cleanly on a fresh install.

Thanks very much!

pr_body:
Previously, if a task between ClangFormat and the clang-format cleanup
tasks like Jni threw an error, wpiformat would exit immediately and the
formatting would remain incorrect. Now, the cleanup tasks run immediately
after ClangFormat. Since they don't throw errors, the formatting should remain
consistent even after error conditions.

pr_title: Fixes #152: Fixed assertion error in TimeStats testing on Windows.
pr_body:
Please review and merge.

Details:
* In test_timestats, improved the approach for checking time deltas such
  that the assertion error now shows the actual and expected delta, instead
  of just the mismatch between true and false.
* In test_timestats, increased the allowable time delta and the measured times
  in order to accomodate the fact that `time.time()` which is used by the `TimeStats`
  class, has only a 1/60 sec precision on Windows. This is a circumvention - the real
  solution would be to use some other service that returns high precision  timings
  on all supported operating systems.

pr_title: Implementing API correct groupby with aggregation methods
pr_body:
<!--
Thank you for your contribution!

Please review https://github.com/ray-project/ray/blob/master/CONTRIBUTING.rst before opening a pull request.
-->
## What do these changes do?
Adds groupby and allows users to interact with the GroupBy object the same way they would in Pandas. 

- `groupby` implementation
- `DataFrameGroupBy` object
- `agg` / `aggregate` / `apply` for non-dictionary arguments
- Some performance improvements overall.

## What do these changes do?

The task dependency manager is used to make any dependencies required by a task available, either by fetching it from a remote node, or eventually, by reconstructing it. This was previously used for scheduling submitted tasks with object arguments, but the logic needed for a task that calls `ray.get` is similar. This PR directs that logic through the task dependency manager as well. This also includes some cleanup in the `SchedulingQueue` code.

pr_title: Fix interoperability with native async generators
pr_body:
- Revigorate support for using the native AsyncGenWrappedValue type as our wrapper type on CPython 3.6+. Instead of trying to manually construct the object using ctypes, trick the interpreter into doing the wrapping/unwrapping for us.
- Add support for PEP 525 async generator hooks (like `sys.set_asyncgen_hooks()`); on 3.6+, our hooks are the same as the system ones, and regardless of version they can be accessed via `async_generator.set_asyncgen_hooks()` and so on
- Add the ability for `@async_generator` to produce a native async generator. Automatically detect whether this is possible (we need to be on CPython 3.6+ and the async function we were given needs to only ever return None). Since there are a few minor semantic differences, native mode currently must be requested using `@async_generator_native`, and plain `@async_generator` warns on code whose behavior would differ with _native. In a future release we can make _native the default, and require people who really need ag_running to work correctly (for example) to use `@async_generator_legacy`.

Release support for allowing the expansion of optional vertex fields.
Fixes #191

Context:
Getting errors when running [`before_request`](https://github.com/uber-common/opentracing-python-instrumentation/blob/master/opentracing_instrumentation/http_server.py#L35) on a tornado request that has unicode characters in the URL. 

From digging into tornado source, it looks like here they convert the url to `latin1`:
https://github.com/tornadoweb/tornado/blob/023a9ac87aa2de87585b71b8fee213346f4b590c/tornado/wsgi.py#L193-L194

https://github.com/tornadoweb/tornado/blob/023a9ac87aa2de87585b71b8fee213346f4b590c/tornado/wsgi.py#L55-L62

So the `http.url` being set as a tag in the opentracing instrumentation is actually a `latin1` encoding of those UTF-8 characters. Then, when we try and cast it to the future `str`, It tries to decode again it appears, resulting in:
`UnicodeDecodeError: 'ascii' codec can't decode byte 0xe9 in position 3: ordinal not in range(128)`

I have recreated essentially what tornado is doing in the test cases here and put together a really quick solution. We would almost certainly want to factor this string handling out of `set_tag` but I will let someone more familiar with this codebase give me guidance on where that should go.

This PR enables python-gitlab to wait if the GitLab rate-limit is hit.
It waits as long as GitLab specifies via the [Retry-After header](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/HTTP/Headers/Retry-After)

GitLab lacks documentation about this feature, but rate-limits can be set in the Admin UI (see screenshot)
and via the settings API.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/6639323/38921537-4fb45bdc-42f6-11e8-8bf1-397dff48c7a4.png)

```json
{
 "throttle_authenticated_api_enabled": false,
 "throttle_authenticated_api_requests_per_period": 2,
 "throttle_authenticated_api_period_in_seconds": 10,
}
```

It can be disabled using the `obey_rate_limit=False` parameter

本 PR 改进了若干个 Python 开发的规范。



## 优化mock模块安装和导入

改进如下：

- mock依赖不写入 requirements.txt 文件
- Python3.4+ 以上，直接使用 `unittest.mock` 模块，无需额外安装
- Python2.7环境，请自行手动安装（`pip install mock`）
- travis CI 环境默认已经安装 mock，在 Python2和3环境中，无需额外安装操作

## 统一文件读写

在Python语言中，基于编码和IO作出以下规范：

- 全部文件均使用 utf-8 编码，包括外部html、js 文件。
- 代码文件首行使用 `# coding=utf-8` 表明该文件编码
- 由于 pyecharts 不会处理到二进制数据，因此文件读写模式均不应当出现 `b`
- 使用 `codecs.open` 函数读写文件
  - 输入输出均为 Unicode 字符串
  - 编码均为统一为 `'utf-8'`，不应当使用其他字符形式如“UTF-8” 、"utf8"等 


## 测试数据路径变更

- 将 json 文件移动到 *test* 文件夹下。
- 将 *json* 文件夹名称更改为更为符合“测试数据”的 *fixtures* 

## 删除 `constants.PAGE_TITLE` 变量

该字符串无特定意义，无需单独设置常量，直接使用字面量形式，可减少import语句

pr_title: Simple RPC based service for PyZMQ
pr_body:
This adds an rpc subpackage to zmq and implements a "simple" RPC service.  This version is focused only on the request/reply pattern, but handles load balancing using ROUTER/DEALER sockets.  While we definitely want to explore richer RPC APIs that allow for arbitrary socket types and message patterns, this version is very useful because of its extreme simplicity and good performance..

Still working on:

* Making the serialization approach pluggable.  I use pickle now, but will allow json and other approaches.
* Adding an async client example.

Needs review:

* Python3 compliance.
* General API choices.
